text: Greg Abbott is the Governor of Texas and a Republican statewide officeholder. Before becoming governor, he served as Texas attorney general, on the Supreme Court of Texas, and as a state district judge. His public profile is closely tied to executive authority, legal strategy, border policy, and the state's relationship with the federal government. He remains a central figure in Texas Republican politics.

Dan Patrick is the Lieutenant Governor of Texas and a Republican statewide officeholder. In that role, he presides over the Texas Senate and has major influence over which legislation moves through the chamber. Before winning statewide office, Patrick served in the Texas Senate and built a public profile in radio and television. His political brand is closely associated with conservative legislative priorities and Republican primary politics.

Beto O'Rourke is a Democratic politician from El Paso who served in the US House of Representatives. Before Congress, he served on the El Paso City Council and built a profile around border, veterans, and civic issues. He later became a statewide Democratic nominee in Texas and a national campaign figure. His Texas political profile is tied to high-visibility statewide campaigns and grassroots organizing.

Wendy Davis is a Democratic attorney and former Texas state senator from Fort Worth. She served in local office before entering the Texas Senate, where she became a prominent voice on education, health care, and women's rights. Davis later ran for governor and remained active in legal and advocacy work. Her public profile is closely tied to state legislative debates and Democratic statewide politics.

Ken Paxton is the Attorney General of Texas and a Republican statewide officeholder. Before becoming attorney general, he served in the Texas House and Texas Senate and worked as an attorney. His office represents Texas in litigation, issues legal opinions, and handles consumer protection and child support enforcement. Paxton is a highly visible Republican legal official in state and national politics.

Christi Craddick is a Republican member of the Railroad Commission of Texas. The commission regulates the state's oil and gas sector and related energy infrastructure. Craddick is an attorney from Midland with a background in energy, law, and statewide campaigns. Her public role sits at the intersection of Texas energy regulation and Republican statewide politics.

Matthew M. Phelan is better known publicly as Dade Phelan, a Republican member of the Texas House from Southeast Texas. He has served as Speaker of the Texas House and as a committee leader on state affairs, natural resources, and budgeting issues. Outside the Legislature, Phelan has worked in commercial real estate development. His profile is tied to House leadership, Gulf Coast regional issues, and intraparty Republican debates.

Don Huffines is a Republican businessman and former Texas state senator from Dallas. He served in the Texas Senate after a career in real estate development. Huffines later became a statewide Republican candidate and a frequent voice in conservative Texas politics. His public profile is tied to property, transportation, fiscal policy, and Republican primary campaigns.

William H. White is better known as Bill White, a Democratic businessman and former mayor of Houston. Before city office, he worked in federal energy policy and in the private energy sector. As mayor, White built a reputation around city operations, fiscal management, and disaster response. He later became the Democratic nominee for governor of Texas.

David M. Middleton II is better known as Mayes Middleton, a Republican member of the Texas Senate. He previously served in the Texas House and has worked in oil, ranching, and business interests along the Gulf Coast. In the Legislature, Middleton has been associated with conservative fiscal and governance issues. His profile in state politics has expanded beyond his Senate seat through statewide Republican campaigning.

George P. Bush is a Republican attorney and former Texas Land Commissioner. Before statewide office, he worked in law, education, business, and the US Navy Reserve. As land commissioner, his work centered on public lands, veterans programs, disaster recovery, and stewardship of the Alamo. He later sought higher statewide office within the Republican Party.

Glenn Hegar is a Republican attorney and former Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts. Before statewide office, he served in both chambers of the Texas Legislature. As comptroller, he was associated with state revenue estimates, tax administration, and oversight of state finances. He later moved into higher education leadership as chancellor of the Texas A&M University System.

Kirk Watson is the mayor of Austin and a former Texas state senator. He has served Austin in citywide office across separate periods, with a public profile centered on transportation, city management, and regional growth. In the Texas Senate, Watson was associated with health care, higher education, and Central Texas infrastructure. He is an attorney and former state attorney-general candidate.

John Whitmire is the mayor of Houston and a former Democratic state senator. Before becoming mayor, he built a long career in the Texas Legislature, including work in both chambers. His legislative profile was closely tied to criminal justice, public safety, and state budget issues. As mayor, he has focused on city operations, policing, and Houston's fiscal pressures.

Robert D. Bullock was a Democratic Texas public official better known as Bob Bullock. He served as lieutenant governor, comptroller, secretary of state, and a member of the Texas House. Bullock was known for a forceful style of legislative negotiation and for shaping state finance and tax administration. His name remains attached to Texas political history and to the state history museum in Austin.

Judith Zaffirini is a Democratic member of the Texas Senate from South Texas. Before and alongside her legislative career, she worked in communications, education, and public service. In the Senate, she has been associated with higher education, health and human services, and budget issues. Her profile is tied to Laredo, border-region policy, and long-running committee work.

Dawn Buckingham is the Texas Land Commissioner and a Republican statewide officeholder. She is a physician who previously served in the Texas Senate before moving to the General Land Office. Her agency work centers on public lands, veterans programs, coastal recovery, disaster response, and the Alamo. Her political profile combines medical, legislative, and statewide executive experience.

Juan "Chuy" Hinojosa is a Democratic member of the Texas Senate from South Texas. He is an attorney and Marine veteran who previously served in the Texas House. In the Legislature, Hinojosa has worked on criminal justice, transportation, higher education, and border-region infrastructure. His public profile is closely connected to the Rio Grande Valley, Corpus Christi, and South Texas state policy.

Morgan Meyer is a Republican member of the Texas House from Dallas. He is an attorney who has chaired the House Ways and Means Committee. In the Legislature, Meyer has worked on tax policy, public finance, and laws addressing human trafficking and child protection. His public profile is tied to Dallas-area representation and House fiscal policy.

Tom Craddick is a Republican member of the Texas House from Midland and a former Speaker of the Texas House. He has been a major figure in West Texas politics and in the rise of the modern Texas Republican Party. His legislative work has often intersected with energy, transportation, ethics, and House procedure. Outside the Capitol, Craddick has worked in business and remains closely identified with the Permian Basin.
Todd A. Hunter is a Republican who serves as a State Representative in the Texas Legislature. He has previously held local public office and performs legislative duties on behalf of his constituents.
Charles L. Geren is a Republican State Representative in the Texas state legislature. He serves as a state elected official working on legislative matters for his Texas constituency.

Jane Nelson is the Texas Secretary of State and a Republican public official. Before joining the executive branch, she served in the Texas Senate and was known for work on budget, health, and education issues. Her current office oversees elections administration, business filings, state records, and international protocol functions for Texas.

Robert Lee Nichols is a Republican member of the Texas State Senate representing parts of East Texas. He serves as a state senator in the Texas Legislature, participating in lawmaking and committee work on behalf of his constituents.

Royce West is a Democratic State Senator serving in the Texas state legislature. He represents constituents in the Texas State Senate and serves on legislative committees to consider and shape state law.

Angie C. Button is a Republican member of the Texas House of Representatives. She serves as a state representative in the Texas legislature and participates in drafting and reviewing state legislation and serving constituents.

R. Christopher Bell is better known as Chris Bell, a Democratic attorney and former Houston public official. He served on Houston City Council and in the US House before becoming a Texas gubernatorial nominee. His public profile is tied to ethics, urban policy, and Democratic statewide campaigns.

sources.title: Biographical Directory of the United States Congress

text: John J. Carona is a former Texas state senator who represented a district in the Dallas area. While in office he served on legislative committees and drew on a background in the private sector to work on business and education-related matters.

Jerry E. Patterson is an American politician who served as Texas Land Commissioner and as a statewide officeholder in Texas. He previously served in the Texas Legislature and has been active in state public affairs.
Dustin R. Burrows is a Republican State Representative serving in the Texas Legislature. He participates in legislative and committee work in the Texas House on behalf of his constituents.

Richard E. Pena Raymond is a Democratic State Representative in the Texas Legislature who serves in the Texas state government. A physician by training, he has emphasized healthcare and public health matters during his legislative service.

Trenton E. Ashby is a Republican State Representative in the Texas Legislature. He serves as a member of the Texas House of Representatives and participates in legislative committees and lawmaking for the state.
Jeff C. Leach is a State Representative serving in the Texas state legislature. He is a Republican public official representing constituents in the Texas state government.

D. Bryan Hughes is a Texas state senator serving in the Texas Legislature as a Republican. He previously served in the Texas House of Representatives and serves on legislative committees in the state senate.

Dennis H. Bonnen is a Texas state legislator who served as Speaker of the Texas House of Representatives. He represented constituents in the Texas legislature and held leadership responsibilities within the chamber.

Lois W. Kolkhorst is a Texas state senator who serves in the Texas Legislature as a Republican. She previously served in the Texas House of Representatives and has held leadership roles on legislative committees.

E. Jeffrey Wentworth is a Republican attorney and former Texas legislator from the San Antonio area. He served in the Texas House and later in the Texas Senate. His legislative profile included transportation, local government, open records, and state institutional issues.

Michael E. Collier is better known as Mike Collier, a Democratic accountant, business executive, and Texas statewide candidate. He has run for lieutenant governor and comptroller while emphasizing fiscal management and public-school finance. His public profile is tied to Democratic efforts to compete for statewide executive offices in Texas.

Phil King is a Republican member of the Texas Senate and a former longtime member of the Texas House. He is an attorney, former police officer, and Texas State Guard officer from North Texas. His legislative profile is tied to energy, criminal justice, telecommunications, and conservative state policy.

George W. Bush is a Republican former Governor of Texas and former President of the United States. Before national office, he worked in energy, baseball ownership, and Republican politics in Texas. His governorship focused on education, tort reform, criminal justice, and executive leadership before his presidential campaign.

Ryan Sitton is an engineer and former member of the Texas Railroad Commission, the state agency that regulates aspects of the oil and gas industry. He has worked in the energy sector and has been a candidate for statewide office in Texas.

Phillip Wayne Huffines is a Dallas businessman and Republican political figure. He has been active in Dallas County Republican Party leadership and in Texas conservative politics. His public profile is connected to real estate, party organization, and the Huffines family political network.
Kelton G. Seliger is a Republican State Senator in the Texas Legislature. He serves in the state senate and participates in legislative committees and processes impacting state policy.

Eduardo A. Lucio Jr is a Texas state senator who has represented constituents in the state legislature. He served as a member of the Texas Senate and held leadership roles on several legislative committees.

Christopher G. Turner is a Texas State Representative serving in the Texas House as a member of the Democratic Party. He has served in House Democratic leadership and works on legislation and constituent services for his district.

Stephen P. Allison is better known as Steve Allison, a Republican former member of the Texas House from San Antonio. Before legislative office, he worked as an attorney and served in civic and education roles. His public profile is tied to public education, local governance, and moderate Republican politics in Bexar County.

Walter W. Christian is a Republican who serves as a Railroad Commissioner for the state of Texas. He previously held elected office in the Texas Legislature and works on matters related to energy regulation and utilities.
Craig L. Estes is a State Senator in the Texas state legislature. He has served on legislative committees and represented constituents from his region in the Texas State Senate.

Richard Friedman is better known as Kinky Friedman, a writer, musician, humorist, and Texas political candidate. He ran for governor as an independent and later sought statewide office as a Democrat. His public profile mixed entertainment, satire, animal welfare, and outsider political campaigns.

Sid Miller is the Texas Agriculture Commissioner, responsible for overseeing the state's agriculture department and related programs. He previously served in the Texas House of Representatives before being elected to his current statewide office.

Trey Martinez Fischer is a State Representative in the Texas legislature and a Democratic public official based in San Antonio. He serves in the Texas state government and has held leadership roles within the Democratic caucus.
Angela Paxton is a Republican who serves as a Texas State Senator. She represents constituents in the Texas Legislature and participates in state lawmaking and constituent services.
Carlos I. Uresti is an American attorney and former member of the Texas State Senate. He served as a Texas state legislator and has practiced law in Texas.

Peter P. Flores is better known as Pete Flores, a Republican member of the Texas Senate. Before legislative service, he worked for Texas Parks and Wildlife and rose through the state game-warden ranks. His public profile is tied to border-region representation, law enforcement, natural resources, and rural Texas issues.

Drew Darby is a State Representative in the Texas House of Representatives. He serves as a Republican legislator representing constituents in the Texas state legislature and participates in crafting and voting on state laws.

Sarah M. Davis is a Republican attorney and former member of the Texas House from Houston. She built a profile as a more independent Republican voice on health care, ethics, and social-policy issues. After leaving the Legislature, she remained active in public affairs, law, and advocacy connected to Texas politics.

Nathaniel W. Parker IV, known as Tan Parker, is a Republican State Senator in the Texas state legislature. He previously served in the Texas House of Representatives and has been active on matters related to business and local governance.

Konni L. Burton is a former Texas state senator who served in the Texas Legislature representing parts of the Fort Worth area. During her time in office she participated in legislative committees and authored and sponsored bills on state policy matters.

John Lujan III is a Republican member of the Texas House from the San Antonio area. Before legislative office, he worked as a firefighter, sheriff's deputy, and small-business owner. His public profile is tied to public safety, workforce issues, and competitive South Texas legislative politics.

John L. Kuempel is a State Representative serving in the Texas House of Representatives. He represents constituents in the Texas state government and participates in the legislative process, including sponsoring and voting on legislation and serving on legislative committees.

David H. Cain was a Democratic member of the Texas Legislature. He served in both the Texas House and Texas Senate, representing North and East Texas communities. His legislative profile included economic development, state affairs, civil justice, and local-government issues.

Sylvester Turner is an American attorney and politician who served as a member of the Texas House of Representatives. He later became mayor of Houston and has worked in municipal government and legal practice.

Marc Andrew LaHood is a Republican member of the Texas House from San Antonio. He is an attorney with a background in criminal defense and local civic work. His public profile is tied to Bexar County politics, House Republican debates, and legal issues at the state Capitol.

Justin A. Holland is a State Representative serving in the Texas House of Representatives. He represents constituents in the Texas state legislature and participates in drafting and voting on state laws.
Carol Alvarado is a Texas State Senator serving in the state legislature. A member of the Democratic Party, she represents constituents in Texas state government and works on legislative matters.

Senfronia Thompson is a Democratic State Representative serving in the Texas House of Representatives. She is a longtime legislator known for her work on state legislation and for providing constituent services in the Texas state government.
Jose Menendez is a Texas state senator who represents constituents in the Texas Legislature. He is a member of the Democratic Party.

James E. "Pete" Laney is a Texas public official who served as Speaker of the Texas House of Representatives and as a longtime member of the Texas Legislature. He is known for his legislative leadership and service in state government.
Nathan M. Johnson is a State Senator in the Texas Legislature and a member of the Democratic Party. He serves in state lawmaking for Texas and works professionally as an attorney.

Lacey M. Hull is a Republican State Representative in the Texas House of Representatives. She serves as a state legislator, participating in the development of state law and representing her Texas constituents.
Rodney G. Ellis is a Texas state senator serving in the Texas Legislature. He previously served as a Harris County commissioner and as a member of the Texas House of Representatives.

Lynn D. Stucky is a State Representative serving in the Texas House of Representatives. He is a licensed veterinarian who has worked on legislation related to agriculture, public health, and rural issues.

Kenneth K. Brimer Jr is a State Senator in the Texas state legislature. He serves as an elected public official participating in legislative work and committee responsibilities on behalf of his constituents.

Ryan A. Guillen is a State Representative in the Texas Legislature and serves as a Republican officeholder representing communities in the Rio Grande Valley region. He participates in legislative duties and committee work on behalf of his constituents.

Gary Gates Jr. is a Republican member of the Texas House from Fort Bend County. He is a businessman with a background in real estate and property operations. His public profile is tied to housing, appropriations, local-government finance, and suburban Republican politics.

Walter T. Price IV is better known as Four Price, a Republican former member of the Texas House from Amarillo. He is an attorney who represented Panhandle communities in the Legislature. His public profile is tied to health care, mental health, regional infrastructure, and House leadership debates.
Leticia Van De Putte is an American politician who served as a member of the Texas State Senate. She has also held statewide office in Texas and been active in state legislative affairs.

Charles L. Perry is a Republican State Senator in the Texas Legislature who represents constituents at the state level. He previously served in the Texas House of Representatives and participates in drafting and evaluating state legislation.

Garnet Coleman is a State Representative in the Texas state legislature representing Houston-area constituents. He serves in the Texas House of Representatives and participates in legislative committees and sessions on behalf of his jurisdiction.

Joe R. Straus III is an American politician who served as Speaker of the Texas House of Representatives and as a member of the Texas Legislature. He represented a San Antonio-area district in the Texas House and led the chamber as its Speaker, overseeing legislative operations.

Kenneth P. King is a Texas state representative serving in the Texas House of Representatives. He is a Republican lawmaker who represents constituents at the state level and participates in the legislative process on behalf of Texas residents.

Abel Herrero is a State Representative in the Texas Legislature who represents constituents at the state level. He is an attorney by profession and serves on legislative committees while handling constituent services and statewide policy matters.
Stephanie D. Klick is a State Representative serving in the Texas state legislature. In her public role she works on state legislative matters and serves on legislative committees.
Kevin P. Eltife is a former member of the Texas State Senate. He previously served as mayor of Tyler and has been active in business and civic affairs in the state.

Craig Goldman is a State Representative in the Texas House of Representatives. He serves as a member of the Texas state legislature, representing his community and performing legislative duties on behalf of his constituents.

David Sibley is a Republican former Texas state senator and former mayor of Waco. Before and after legislative office, he worked in law, dentistry, and public affairs. His legislative profile included business regulation, telecommunications, electric utility policy, and Central Texas regional issues.

Dan Patrick is the Lieutenant Governor of Texas, presiding over the Texas Senate and serving as a statewide elected official. He served in the Texas Senate and worked as a broadcaster and media personality before holding statewide office.

Aaron F. Reitz is a Texas attorney who served as Deputy Attorney General for Legal Strategy in the Office of the Texas Attorney General and later served as chief of staff to a US senator. He has also held a leadership role at the US Department of Justice and remains active in statewide legal and public affairs.

Ann Johnson is a Democratic State Representative in the Texas House of Representatives. She serves in the Texas state legislature, working on legislation and representing constituents in her jurisdiction.

Janie Lopez is a Republican state representative serving in the Texas House of Representatives. She represents a district in south Texas and carries out legislative duties on behalf of her constituents at the state level.

Adan Hinojosa is publicly known as Adam Hinojosa, a Republican member of the Texas Senate from South Texas. He is a small-business owner from the Corpus Christi area. His public profile is tied to economic development, energy, ports, agriculture, and Republican organizing in the Rio Grande Valley and Coastal Bend.

Allen B. West is an American politician, attorney, and retired US Army lieutenant colonel who served as a US Representative and remains active in Texas politics. He serves as a County Party Chair in Texas state and participates in party leadership and public commentary.
Robert L. Duncan is a State Senator in the Texas state legislature. He serves as a public official representing constituents and participating in state legislative affairs.

Chrysta Castañeda is a Dallas attorney, engineer, and Democratic candidate for the Texas Railroad Commission. Her legal work has focused on oil and gas, energy markets, and technical litigation. Her public profile is tied to energy regulation, flaring, mineral rights, and Democratic statewide campaigns.

Drew Alan Springer Jr is a Texas state legislator. He serves in the Texas Legislature representing constituents at the state level and participates in legislative committees and duties related to state policy.
Florence Shapiro is a former Texas state senator who served in the state legislature. She previously served as mayor of Plano and has been active in local government and public service.
Jacey R. Jetton is a State Representative in the Texas House of Representatives. He serves as a legislator in the Texas state government, participating in lawmaking and constituent services.

Matt F. Shaheen is a Republican who serves as a State Representative in the Texas Legislature. He represents constituents in the state and participates in legislative committees and policymaking on state issues.

Justin B. Busby is a justice of the Supreme Court of Texas. Before joining the state's highest civil court, he served as an appellate judge and worked in appellate law. His public profile is tied to civil appeals, judicial administration, and statewide judicial elections.
Robert L. Hall III is a State Senator in the Texas Legislature. He represents constituents in the state Senate and serves on legislative committees involved in state governance.
